sections.title: Performance Analysis — Distill (book_shorts)
sections.content: This is a static, file-based SPA (no DB), so "N+1 queries" show up as **repeated O(n) linear scans** and **unmemoized recomputation** instead. Findings below are ranked by impact, with file:line references and fixes.

sections.title: 1. N+1-style lookup patterns (linear scans that should be O(1))
2. Unnecessary React re-renders
sections.content:
**Biggest issue — `BookCard` subscribes to the whole progress store** (`src/pages/Library.tsx:30`):

```tsx
function BookCard({ book, step }: { book: Book; step?: number }) {
  const all = useAllProgress()      // subscribes to EVERY book's progress
  const progress = all[book.id]
```

Every `BookCard` on the Library page (all tracks + all books) calls `useAllProgress()`. Since `store.ts` fires all listeners on any `set()`, marking one chapter read in one book re-renders **every card on the page**, including unrelated ones. Fix: scope the subscription to the one book that matters.

```tsx
import { useBookProgress } from '../lib/progress'

function BookCard({ book, step }: { book: Book; step?: number }) {
  const progress = useBookProgress(book.id)   // only this book's slice
  const pct = completionPct(book.map.chapters.length, progress)
  ...
```

**`MapChapterCard` re-renders and re-parses Markdown for all chapters on any single toggle** (`src/pages/BookMap.tsx:18-67`, `158-166`): `BookMap` subscribes via `useBookProgress(bookId)`, so toggling one chapter's read state re-renders the whole tree, and every `MapChapterCard`'s `<Markdown>` re-runs `marked.parse` even though `chapter.summary` is unchanged. Fix: memoize the card.

```tsx
import { memo } from 'react'

const MapChapterCard = memo(function MapChapterCard({ bookId, chapter, read, available }: {...}) {
  ...
})
```

**`ContinueStrip`** (`src/pages/Library.tsx:58`) also calls `useAllProgress()` just to find the single most-recent entry — same over-subscription problem, though lower impact since it's one component, not N.

sections.title: 3. Caching opportunities
4. Memory leaks
sections.content:
No real leaks found — every `addEventListener` in `Reader.tsx` (scroll: `:65`, keydown: `:83`) and `Library.tsx` (`:105`) has a matching cleanup, and the async chapter-load effect (`Reader.tsx:30-51`) correctly guards against a stale `.then()` firing after unmount with the `alive` flag. `store.ts`'s `subscribe`/unsubscribe pair is also correctly wired through `useSyncExternalStore`.

One minor churn issue rather than a leak: `Reader.tsx:59-68`'s scroll listener effect depends on `[state]` and `:70-85`'s keydown listener depends on `[..., showSettings]`, so toggling the settings panel tears down and rebinds the global keydown listener unnecessarily. Not harmful, but avoidable — move `showSettings` access into a ref if you want to trim listener churn.

sections.title: 5. Redundant computations
sections.content:
- `searchBooks` (`content.ts:173-193`) rebuilds `weak` (`b.map.chapters.map(...).join(' ').toLowerCase()`) for every book on every keystroke, even though it's static per book. Precompute a lowercase search-index string per book once at load time alongside `bookById`, then just substring-match against it in `searchBooks`.
- `Library.tsx:109-113`'s `totals` `useMemo` is fine (empty deps, runs once), but it calls `bookStats(b)` for every book — once #2's cache lands, this becomes free.

sections.title: Priority order
sections.content:
1. `BookCard` → `useBookProgress(book.id)` instead of `useAllProgress()` (biggest re-render win, one-line-ish change)
2. Memoize `bookStats` (module-level `Map` cache)
3. Cache `loadChapter` results by `${bookId}:${n}`
4. `getBook`/`categoryOf`/`booksInCategory` → `Map`-backed lookups
5. `React.memo` on `MapChapterCard` and `Markdown`
6. Precompute search index for `searchBooks`

Want me to implement these directly?
